method: Parsed SWARM-LANES.md + archive (n=1043 total lanes, 1040 closed, 155 sessions). Coordination surface = WIP × N_domains per session. AUC comparison for merge<80% prediction. Era-controlled (early/mid/late). Pearson r for correlation.

H2_crossover_alignment.prediction: Crossover point at CS ~20-30 aligns with L-629 ceiling
H2_crossover_alignment.result: CS 26-36 merge=55.4%, CS 37+ merge=63.2% — but driven by early-era sessions (CS_avg=941). Late-era CS range (4-25) is flat at 84-97% merge. No clean crossover within mature era.

H3_scale_invariance.prediction: r(CS, merge) stronger than r(WIP, merge)

findings: n=1040 closed lanes across 155 sessions. Coordination surface (WIP*N_domains) DOES NOT improve quality prediction over raw WIP.
H1 FALSIFIED: AUC delta = -0.0171 (CS=0.7173 vs WIP=0.7344). CS is WORSE, not better.
H2 FALSIFIED: Crossover at CS=26-36 is entirely early-era (CS_avg=941.4). Late-era CS range (4-25) shows flat 84-97% merge — no crossover.
H3 FALSIFIED: r(CS, merge) = -0.0945 vs r(WIP, merge) = -0.1687. WIP captures MORE variance, not less.
Domain diversity gradient weak: 1-domain 93%, 3-domain 82%, 4-7 domain 86-93%. Vanishes after era control.

Root cause: era/protocol maturity (L-601 enforcement) dominates all continuous topology metrics. Fractal boundary-growth metaphor does not apply — quality degradation is driven by discrete enforcement transitions, not continuous complexity growth.
Degraded sessions (merge<80%): 27/155 (17.4%). All 3 hypotheses FALSIFIED — strongest negative result in fractals domain.
